Title: Optimization of carbon and nitrogen utilization for enhanced photosynthesis, biomass and yield by coexpression of ZmPEPC, GmAspAT and NtGS in aspartate aminotransferase Brassica juncea

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: carbon assimilation / anaplerotic carbon metabolism linked to carbon-nitrogen metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Coexpression including ZmPEPC increased PEPC activity and improved photosynthetic rate, sugar/starch accumulation, biomass, seed yield and oil content while lowering ammonium and stress markers in Brassica juncea.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: 1H NMR metabolomics; GC-MS fatty acid profiling; no accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgene expressed in T2/T3 Brassica lines; functional assays performed mainly in leaves of 45-day-old plants
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ambient CO2 (~400 ppm); low CO2 (~200 ppm); nitrogen supply 6, 3, or 0.3 mM
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: stable coexpression in transgenic Brassica; RT-PCR; enzyme activity assay; chlorophyll fluorescence; gas exchange; metabolite profiling; seed yield analysis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: total soluble sugars; starch; amino acids; seed oil
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: leaves for sugars/starch/amino acids; seeds for oil
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports photosynthesis, biomass accumulation, seed yield and nutritional oil production
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful in multigene engineering to improve carbon use efficiency, nitrogen use efficiency, biomass and seed yield under low CO2 and low N; should be stacked with downstream N-assimilation genes rather than expressed alone.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmPEPC was one of three coexpressed metabolic genes in Brassica juncea cv. Varuna. 2. BjPAG lines showed 102%–134% higher total PEPC activity than vector control. 3. Coexpression enhanced CO2 assimilation, chlorophyll fluorescence traits, carbohydrates, biomass, seed yield and oil content under ambient/low CO2 and multiple N supplies.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper emphasizes PEPC alone may cause malate accumulation or growth defects in some C3 hosts; balanced coexpression with AspAT and GS is presented as the key strategy.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No growth penalty reported in BjPAG lines; discussion notes potential negative effects when PEPC is overexpressed alone.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: transaminates oxaloacetate using glutamate to produce aspartate and 2-oxoglutarate
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Coexpression including GmAspAT increased AspAT activity and promoted amino acid accumulation, improved photosynthesis, biomass and seed yield, and reduced ammonium accumulation in Brassica juncea.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: 1H NMR metabolomics; GC-MS fatty acid profiling; no accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgene expressed in T2/T3 Brassica lines; functional assays mainly in leaves of 45-day-old plants
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ambient CO2 (~400 ppm); low CO2 (~200 ppm); nitrogen supply 6, 3, or 0.3 mM
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: stable coexpression in transgenic Brassica; RT-PCR; enzyme activity assay; NMR metabolomics; physiology and yield measurements
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: aspartate-family amino acids; sugars; seed oil
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: leaves for amino acids and sugars; seeds for oil
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports nitrogen assimilation, primary metabolism, biomass accumulation and seed yield
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Best deployed with PEPC and GS to channel oxaloacetate and amino groups into aspartate-family metabolism and improve NUE/yield under N limitation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmAspAT was coexpressed with ZmPEPC and NtGS in Brassica juncea. 2. BjPAG lines showed 50%–74% higher total AspAT activity than vector control. 3. Coexpression was associated with higher aspartate and multiple amino acids, improved photosynthesis and biomass, and increased seed yield under varied CO2 and N conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussion states AspAT overexpression alone previously did not improve NUE or yield, supporting need for cooperative action with PEPC and GS.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No tradeoff reported in coexpression lines; limited benefit expected if expressed alone based on cited prior studies.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: ligates ammonium to glutamate in an ATP-dependent reaction to produce glutamine
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Coexpression including NtGS increased GS activity, reduced free ammonium, enhanced amino acid and carbohydrate accumulation, improved photosynthesis, biomass, seed yield and oil content in Brassica juncea.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for ammonium reassimilation is supported in discussion, but not directly established as sole bottleneck in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: 1H NMR metabolomics; GC-MS fatty acid profiling; no accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Described in discussion as cytosolic GS important for nitrogen remobilization from senescing leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgene expressed in T2/T3 Brassica lines; functional assays mainly in leaves of 45-day-old plants
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ambient CO2 (~400 ppm); low CO2 (~200 ppm); nitrogen supply 6, 3, or 0.3 mM
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: stable coexpression in transgenic Brassica; RT-PCR; enzyme activity assay; ammonium measurement; NMR metabolomics; physiological and yield analysis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: glutamine; amino acids; sugars; seed oil
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: leaves for ammonium/amino acids/sugars; seeds for oil
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: improves nitrogen use efficiency, supports amino acid synthesis, biomass accumulation and seed/oil yield
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising component for improving NUE and grain/seed productivity, particularly when stacked with carbon-skeleton supplying genes under low-N or changing CO2 environments.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NtGS was coexpressed with ZmPEPC and GmAspAT in Brassica juncea. 2. BjPAG lines showed 33%–47% higher total GS activity and 21%–32% lower ammonium than vector control. 3. Coexpression improved photosynthesis, metabolites, biomass and seed yield across CO2 and N treatments.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper frames NtGS as cytosolic GS aiding reassimilation of photorespired NH3 and balancing C/N metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No penalty reported; possible metabolic imbalance not observed in coexpression lines.
